subject: Nationwide Claim 000883-GR — Kevin Melton — Full Denial
adjuster: Ryan Graham, FL Lic W848638, Nationwide Claims Associate (W 866-425-9200 x15638)
forwardedBy: Kerouls Gayed (Roof-ER): 'This is a denial from Kevin Melton.'
denialText: Greetings Kevin, This is Ryan from Nationwide Insurance. Per our conversation I've attached a full denial letter that goes into more detail on why we were unable to provide coverage for the damages found. I will close the claim (as is part of our claims process), but as mentioned in our call, if you have any questions, please reach out using my information below!
keyDenialLanguage: attached a full denial letter that goes into more detail on why we were unable to provide coverage for the damages found
I will close the claim (as is part of our claims process)
carrierTactic: Nationwide standard close-after-denial pattern. Specific denial rationale is in the attached PDF (not transcribed in this email). The 'as is part of our claims process' framing presents closing as routine, not adversarial.
patentMapping: Nationwide's claim handling automation. The denial PDF likely follows the same format as the Nationwide PL Property Full Denial captured earlier for KYM GULLY (also under Thomes Buchanan adjuster) — which used wear/tear/deterioration template citation to policy provisions.
lessonForAnalyzer: Nationwide denials always have the substantive content in the attached PDF — the email itself is a thin wrapper. Counter-letter requires homeowner to forward the PDF for analysis. Reach out to Ryan Graham specifically asking for the underlying inspection report + weather data sources.
